angel investors or venture capitalists or seek loans from commercial banks
or financial institutions
✅ WHO
CAN APPLY?
• DPIIT recognised startups.
• Incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application.
• Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with
market fit, viable commercialisation, and scope of scaling. Startup should
be using technology in its core product or service, or business model.
• Startup should not have received more than ₹10 lakh of monetary support
under any other Central or State Government scheme
💰 WHAT DO
YOU GET?
• Up to ₹20 lakh as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype
development, or product trials.
• Up to ₹50 lakh of investment for market entry, commercialisation, or

machinery/equipment.
💰 WHAT DO
YOU GET?
• Grant-in-aid of normally up to ₹50 lakh per approved proposal for up to 18
months.
• Funding is released milestone-wise, generally in 40:40:20 instalments.
• Associated incubators receive an additional 10% of the sanctioned
Government grant share for mentoring, due diligence and monitoring
support.
• Intellectual property generated remains with the innovator/startup or may
be jointly shared with the incubator as per their agreement.
🚀 HOW TO
APPLY
• Apply online through the GREAT portal when applications are invited by
NTTM/Ministry of Textiles.
https://nttm.texmin.gov.in/StartupRegistration/StartupUserRegistration
🔗 KEY
LINKS
• GREAT Application Portal:

• DPIIT recognised early-stage startups with innovative ideas and projects
that use space technology to address challenges in space sector
• Startups should not have received more than ₹50 lakh of monetary
support under any Central or State Government Scheme.
💰 WHAT DO
YOU GET?
• Funding, mentorship, training and networking opportunities.
• Grant of ₹1 crore provided to selected startups on milestone-basis.

APPLY

answer_text:
Based on the information provided, the following grants may be relevant for your AI startup in Pune:

- GREAT (Grant for Early Technology Startups)
- NIDHI-EIR (National Innovation and Digital Infrastructure for Rural Electrification)
- E-YUVA (E-YUVA)
- SITARE (SITARE)
- Atal Tinkering Labs
- DCIS (Defence Common Infrastructure Support)
- IN-SPACe Seed Fund
- RKVY (Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana)
- SPARSH (Skill Development and Entrepreneurship Programme for School Students)
- GREAT (Global Education and Research Alliance for Talent)
- SISFS (Skill Innovation and Startups Fund)
- BIG (Business Incubation and Growth)
- iDEX (Innovation and Incubation Development and Export)
- ADITI (Atal Disinvestment and Innovation Trust)
- PRAYAS 2.0 (Prayas)
- DCIS (Defence Common Infrastructure Support)
- IN-SPACe Seed Fund
- RKVY (Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana)
- SPARSH (Skill Development and Entrepreneurship Programme for School Students)
- GREAT (Global Education and Research Alliance for Talent)
- FFS (Funded Funding Scheme)
- Startup India FoF 2.0 (Startup India FoF)
- AcE (Accelerator and Catalyst for Entrepreneurship)
- AgriSURE (AgriSure)
- BIRAC SEED (Birac Seed Fund)
- LEAP Fund (Leap Fund)
- SAMRIDH (Samridhi)
- Antariksh VCF (Antariksh Venture Capital Fund)
- CGSS (Civil Geotechnical Services)
- CGTMSE (Civil Geotechnical Maintenance and Engineering Services)
- PMMY (Pune Municipal Corporation)
- Stand Up India (Stand Up India)

Please note that the specific grants available may vary based on your startup's needs and eligibility criteria. It is recommended to review the eligibility requirements and application processes for each grant to determine which may be the most suitable for your AI startup in Pune.

Guidelines for Startup India Seed Fund Scheme
Guidelines for Startup India Seed Fund Scheme

1. Introduction
Easy availability of capital is essential for entrepreneurs at the early stages of
growth of an enterprise. Funding from angel investors and venture capital firms
becomes available to startups only after the proof of concept has been provided.
Similarly, banks provide loans only to asset -backed applicants. It is essential to
provide seed funding to startups with an innovativ e idea to conduct proof of
concept trials.
Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) aims to provide financial assistance to

7. Any startup will not receive seed support more than once each as per provisions of guidelines.
6. Eligibility Criteria for Incubators: The eligibility criteria for an incubator to apply in the Startup India
Seed Fund scheme are as follows:
1. Incubator must be a legal entity:
a) A society registered under the Societies Registration Act 1860, or
b) A Trust registered under the Indian Trusts Act 1882, or
c) A Private Limited company registered under the Co mpanies Act 1956 or the Companies Act 2013,
or
d) A statutory body created through an Act of legislature
2. Incubator should be operational for at least two years on the date of application to the scheme
3. Incubator must have facilities to seat at least 25 individuals

3. Eligibility Criteria:
3.1 Eligibility Criteria for Startups
The eligibility criteria for a startup to apply under the Startup India Seed Fund
Scheme shall be as follows:
1. A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at
the time of application
2. Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with
market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling
3. Startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or
business model, or distribution model, or methodology to solve the
problem being targeted

7. Any startup will not receive seed support more than once each as per provisions of guidelines.

The eligibility requirements for the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme are as follows:
1. A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application.
2. Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling.
3. Startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model, or distribution model, or methodology to solve the problem being targeted.

Fund Scheme shall be as follows:
1. A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of applicatio n
2. Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable
commercialization, and scope of scaling
3. Startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model, or distribution
model, or methodology to solve the problem being targeted
4. Preference would be given to startups creating innovative solutions in sectors such as social impact,
waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, agriculture, food processing,
biotechnology, healthcare, energy, mobility, defence, space, railways, oil and gas, textiles, etc.

TIDE 2.0 – Technology Incubation & Development
of Entrepreneurs
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

📌 WHAT IS
THIS?
TIDE 2.0 helps early -stage tech startups get funding, incubation, and
mentoring so they can turn ideas and prototypes into market-ready products.
🎯
OBJECTIVES
• TIDE 2.0 scheme is a fellowship/grant for aspiring entrepreneurs, aiming
to create a holistic ecosystem by providing financial and technical support
to technology startups by creating necessary linkages.
✅ WHO
CAN APPLY?
• Information Technology based, DPIIT-recognised startups.
💰 WHAT DO
YOU GET?
Extended to Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR) at various stages:

2

It is proposed to support such initiatives by providing financial an d policy support for
strengthening technology incubation activity. This would nurture technology innovation
and, in the long run, enable local development of Electronics and IT products and
packages.
5. Implementation Mechanism
The following mechanism will be followed for implementation of the scheme:
5. 1 Invitation of Proposals
The project proposals for TIDE would be invited (as per the enclosed format) from
institutions of higher learning conducting education Programmes of M. Tech/PhD in
Engineering and Technology in ICT, Electronics and Management. These include
institutions such as IITs, IIMs, NITs, IIITs and other premier institutes. The invitation
would also be published on the DeitY website.
